title: Chip Industry Week In Review
gist: A chip-industry news digest reporting a reported Apple-Intel design/manufacturing partnership and a 3M-TPU Intel Foundry deal for Google, Amazon preparing to sell Trainium accelerators externally, Amkor-TSMC US packaging tie-up, Intel 18A-P entering risk production, SK hynix 12-layer HBM4E samples, doubling NOR/SLC NAND prices, and a wave of US/Korea/EU government semiconductor funding.

insights.claim: Trump publicly stated that Apple will partner with Intel on chip design and production in the U.S., a second reported customer win for Intel this month, though Intel has not confirmed it.
insights.speaker: The SE Staff
insights.so_what: An Apple foundry/design win would be a major external-customer validation for Intel Foundry; unconfirmed status makes it a watch item rather than a confirmed catalyst for INTC.
insights.quote: Trump said in a post that Apple will partner with Intel on chip design and production in the U.S., marking a second reported win for the chipmaker this month.

insights.claim: Intel Foundry will reportedly manufacture 3 million TPUs for Google, an external-customer volume win that Intel has not confirmed.
insights.speaker: The SE Staff
insights.so_what: A 3M-unit Google TPU build would materially fill Intel Foundry capacity and signal hyperscaler trust in Intel process tech; track for confirmation as an INTC foundry-thesis input.
insights.quote: Intel Foundry will also reportedly manufacture 3 million TPUs for Google. Intel has not confirmed either deal.

insights.claim: Intel Foundry's 18A-P process has entered risk production with improvements over 18A in performance, power, thermal, and via-resistance.
insights.speaker: The SE Staff
insights.so_what: 18A-P reaching risk production keeps Intel's leading-edge roadmap on schedule, supporting the case that Intel can compete for advanced external foundry business.
insights.quote: Intel Foundry said its 18A-P process has entered risk production, with performance, power, thermal, and via-resistance improvements over 18A.
insights.locator: IEEE/JSAP Symposium on VLSI Technology & Circuits

insights.claim: Intel appointed Seok-Hee Lee, the former president and CEO of SK hynix and a former Intel veteran, as EVP of Intel Foundry to lead advanced packaging, system integration, and back-end manufacturing.
insights.speaker: The SE Staff
insights.so_what: Hiring a former SK hynix CEO into Intel Foundry's back-end leadership signals a serious push on advanced packaging, a key battleground against TSMC.

insights.claim: Amazon is reportedly in talks to sell its custom Trainium AI accelerator chips directly to outside companies for use in their own data centers.
insights.speaker: The SE Staff
insights.so_what: If Amazon sells Trainium externally, it becomes a merchant AI-accelerator vendor competing with Nvidia/AMD beyond its own cloud, broadening the custom-silicon threat to GPU incumbents.
insights.quote: Amazon is reportedly in talks to sell its custom Trainium AI accelerator chips directly to outside companies for use in their own data centers.

insights.claim: Amazon's reported move to externalize Trainium follows Google's announcement that it will sell its TPUs commercially, indicating hyperscaler-designed accelerators are becoming merchant products.
insights.speaker: The SE Staff
insights.so_what: Two of the largest hyperscalers opening their in-house accelerators to third parties marks a structural shift that erodes Nvidia's near-monopoly pricing power in AI training silicon.
insights.quote: The move comes on the heels of Google's announcement last month that it will sell its TPUs commercially.

themes.theme: US advanced packaging & supply chain onshoring

insights.claim: Amkor signed a 10-year agreement with TSMC to provide advanced packaging and test services in Arizona, tying TSMC's U.S. fab expansion to domestic OSAT capacity.
insights.speaker: The SE Staff
insights.so_what: A 10-year Amkor-TSMC packaging deal closes the US back-end gap for TSMC's Arizona output and is a durable revenue anchor for Amkor's domestic capacity.
insights.quote: Amkor inked a 10-year agreement with TSMC to provide advanced packaging and test services in Arizona, tying TSMC's U.S. fab expansion to domestic OSAT capacity.

insights.claim: Nokia is expanding its advanced test and packaging operations in Pennsylvania to boost U.S. production of photonic chips and optical modules for AI and telecom infrastructure by 10 times its current level.
insights.speaker: The SE Staff
insights.so_what: A 10x US photonics/optical-module capacity expansion positions Nokia to capture AI-datacenter optical interconnect demand on US soil.
insights.quote: Nokia is expanding its advanced test and packaging operations in Pennsylvania to boost U.S. production of photonic chips and optical modules used in AI and telecom infrastructure by 10 times its current level.

insights.claim: NOR flash and SLC NAND contract prices more than doubled in the first half of 2026, per TrendForce.
insights.speaker: The SE Staff
insights.so_what: A >2x H1 2026 jump in NOR/SLC NAND contract prices signals a tightening specialty-memory market that benefits suppliers' margins and pressures device makers' BOM costs.
insights.quote: NOR flash and SLC NAND contract prices more than doubled in the first half of 2026, reported TrendForce.

insights.claim: SK Hynix shipped samples of its 12-layer HBM4E memory to major customers.
insights.speaker: The SE Staff
insights.so_what: 12-layer HBM4E sampling keeps SK hynix at the front of the HBM roadmap that gates AI-accelerator memory bandwidth, reinforcing its leadership in the highest-margin DRAM segment.

insights.claim: ASML, TSMC, and imec demonstrated a 300mm integration flow for 2D-material nFETs and pFETs at 50nm contacted poly pitch, moving 2D transistors closer to manufacturable post-silicon logic.
insights.speaker: The SE Staff
insights.so_what: A 300mm 2D-material transistor flow is an early but concrete step toward post-silicon channels, a long-dated roadmap signal for the litho/equipment ecosystem.
insights.quote: The work moves 2D transistors closer to manufacturable post-silicon logic.
insights.locator: IEEE/JSAP Symposium on VLSI Technology & Circuits

insights.claim: TSMC presented its A16 angstrom-class GAA CMOS platform with Super Power Rail backside power delivery.
insights.speaker: The SE Staff
insights.so_what: A16 with backside power delivery keeps TSMC's angstrom-era roadmap intact, the benchmark competitors like Intel and Samsung are measured against.
insights.locator: IEEE/JSAP Symposium on VLSI Technology & Circuits

themes.theme: AI in chip design and compute records

insights.claim: CoreWeave set a new MLPerf Training v6.0 record, training DeepSeek-V3 in about two minutes on 8,192 Nvidia GB300 NVL72 GPUs.
insights.speaker: The SE Staff
insights.so_what: A ~2-minute DeepSeek-V3 training run on GB300 hardware showcases CoreWeave's at-scale GB300 deployment and demonstrates Nvidia's newest platform performance for AI-infra buyers.
insights.quote: CoreWeave setting a new record, training DeepSeek-V3 in about two minutes on 8,192 Nvidia GB300 NVL72 GPUs.

insights.claim: A growing set of vendors is targeting AI agents for EDA/chip design, including ChipAgents' Renoir LLM for RTL generation and debugging, Architect Labs (which raised $24M for end-to-end provably-verified chip design), and Nvidia's LLM-based RTL clock-gating work.
insights.speaker: The SE Staff
insights.so_what: LLM-driven RTL/verification tools are becoming a real product category, a potential disruptor and margin opportunity for incumbent EDA vendors (Synopsys, Cadence).

insights.claim: The US government signed a letter of intent for up to $50M to expand Coherent's InP wafer manufacturing in Texas and finalized a $500M award to SandboxAQ for AI-driven semiconductor materials discovery.
insights.speaker: The SE Staff
insights.so_what: Direct federal funding into InP photonics (Coherent) and AI materials discovery (SandboxAQ) channels public capital into AI-supply-chain bottlenecks worth tracking for both names.

insights.claim: The South Korean government launched a ~US$520M program to develop on-device AI chips.
insights.speaker: The SE Staff
insights.so_what: A ~$520M Korean on-device AI program signals state-backed competition in edge-AI silicon, relevant to Korea's fabless and IP ecosystem.

insights.claim: Qualcomm is reportedly in talks to buy Tenstorrent, per The Information.
insights.speaker: The SE Staff
insights.so_what: A Qualcomm-Tenstorrent deal would give Qualcomm a RISC-V/AI-accelerator IP base and signals incumbent SoC vendors buying their way into AI-datacenter silicon.
insights.quote: Qualcomm is reportedly in talks to buy Tenstorrent, per The Information.
insights.locator: Reports and Deals / M&A and IPOs

insights.claim: Tencent-backed AI chip maker Enflame won IPO approval for the Shanghai exchange.
insights.speaker: The SE Staff
insights.so_what: Enflame's Shanghai IPO approval shows China continuing to fund and capitalize domestic AI-accelerator champions despite export controls.
insights.locator: Reports and Deals / M&A and IPOs

insights.claim: Mobileye announced plans to expand its robotaxi business beyond supplying self-driving technology to owning and operating its own autonomous ride-hailing venture.
insights.speaker: The SE Staff
insights.so_what: Mobileye moving from tech supplier to robotaxi operator changes its business model and revenue profile, a re-rating consideration for the stock.

insights.claim: Stellantis, Wayve and Uber announced a partnership to jointly develop and deploy driverless robotaxis, and US legislators are demanding an investigation of Tesla's full self-driving for safety risks.
insights.speaker: The SE Staff
insights.so_what: A Stellantis/Wayve/Uber robotaxi alliance plus regulatory scrutiny of Tesla FSD reshapes the competitive and regulatory landscape for autonomous ride-hailing.

insights.claim: Bloomberg projects 11% year-over-year growth in global EV sales in 2026 and still expects EVs to exceed half of all passenger vehicles by 2035, but cut its long-term estimate amid US and China sales slowdowns.
insights.speaker: The SE Staff
insights.so_what: A reduced long-term EV penetration outlook with US/China slowdowns tempers demand expectations for EV-exposed semis (power devices, BMS, SiC).

insights.claim: KAIST and Georgia Tech engineers demonstrated a manifold microchannel cooler embedded in silicon that dissipated more than 2,000 W/cm² while keeping junction temperature below 100°C.
insights.speaker: The SE Staff
insights.so_what: >2,000 W/cm² embedded cooling addresses the thermal wall limiting high-power AI packages, a key enabler to watch for next-gen accelerator density.
insights.quote: demonstrated a manifold microchannel cooler embedded in silicon that dissipated more than 2,000 W/cm² while keeping junction temperature below 100°C
